replies.msg:
爆呢張相個位巴打真係要俾十萬個正評你
唔係你應該都幾難周到佢身敗名裂

He is my friend, and a bandmate in the past. For some of you that say he can't play, you're wrong. We had a great time playing music together and he was good at the type of music that he liked. I never felt that he was unkind or unpleasant to be with in those years. He was playful and at most I would say that he used to enjoy the spotlight a bit more than I do. We haven't been in touch much since I left HK, but I do catch up with him once in a long while.

A lot of you were trying to get him fired, but you didn't think that getting him fired would not solve the problem. Getting him fired, would actually make things worse for the mother and the kid, but you did it anyway. Look, I'm not trying to convince anyone on who is right or wrong, but if you read their conversation, certainly there is more to the story than that $5000 or "cheating". There is the hacking which seems to have stressed him up, then there is their relationship issue which is really hard for outsider to judge.

People insert lots of information about him cheating, about how he was behaving at work, or how he DM girls on Instagram, and I don't know if they are all real because I couldn't picture him like that and you're not trying to prove it. If everything is true tho, he is a terrible person. However, all the messages I've read are either "my friend told me..." Or "I heard that he....". This is just some BS. Let's talk about facts. Moreover, without saying it is their fault, the girls that knowingly cheat with him are doing so willingly.

I haven't spoke to him yet, but I hope he can hang on, and for those of you that are trying to make things worse should know that you're nobody to judge someone's marriage, decision and their personal matters even if one of them decided to publish it online. Destroying someone's life with so little evidence of wrong doing is just stupid.

Lastly, for those of you that shared his address and phone number and other private information, I hope someone will do that to you too. You can shame someone online, and he could be a terrible father and husband, but he is not a murder.

We haven't been in touch much since I left HK, but I do catch up with him once in a long while.

A lot of you were trying to get him fired, but you didn't think that getting him fired would not solve the problem. Getting him fired, would actually make things worse for the mother and the kid, but you did it anyway. Look, I'm not trying to convince anyone on who is right or wrong, but if you read their conversation, certainly there is more to the story than that $5000 or "cheating". There is the hacking which seems to have stressed him up, then there is their relationship issue which is really hard for outsider to judge.

People insert lots of information about him cheating, about how he was behaving at work, or how he DM girls on Instagram, and I don't know if they are all real because I couldn't picture him like that and you're not trying to prove it. If everything is true tho, he is a terrible person. However, all the messages I've read are either "my friend told me..." Or "I heard that he....". This is just some BS. Let's talk about facts. Moreover, without saying it is their fault, the girls that knowingly cheat with him are doing so willingly.

I haven't spoke to him yet, but I hope he can hang on, and for those of you that are trying to make things worse should know that you're nobody to judge someone's marriage, decision and their personal matters even if one of them decided to publish it online. Destroying someone's life with so little evidence of wrong doing is just stupid.

Lastly, for those of you that shared his address and phone number and other private information, I hope someone will do that to you too. You can shame someone online, and he could be a terrible father and husband, but he is not a murder.

He is my friend, and a bandmate in the past. For some of you that say he can't play, you're wrong. We had a great time playing music together and he was good at the type of music that he liked. quote.msg:
He is my friend, and a bandmate in the past. For some of you that say he can't play, you're wrong. We had a great time playing music together and he was good at the type of music that he liked. I never felt that he was unkind or unpleasant to be with in those years. He was playful and at most I would say that he used to enjoy the spotlight a bit more than I do. We haven't been in touch much since I left HK, but I do catch up with him once in a long while.

A lot of you were trying to get him fired, but you didn't think that getting him fired would not solve the problem. Getting him fired, would actually make things worse for the mother and the kid, but you did it anyway. Look, I'm not trying to convince anyone on who is right or wrong, but if you read their conversation, certainly there is more to the story than that $5000 or "cheating". There is the hacking which seems to have stressed him up, then there is their relationship issue which is really hard for outsider to judge.

People insert lots of information about him cheating, about how he was behaving at work, or how he DM girls on Instagram, and I don't know if they are all real because I couldn't picture him like that and you're not trying to prove it. If everything is true tho, he is a terrible person. However, all the messages I've read are either "my friend told me..." Or "I heard that he....". This is just some BS. Let's talk about facts. Moreover, without saying it is their fault, the girls that knowingly cheat with him are doing so willingly.

I haven't spoke to him yet, but I hope he can hang on, and for those of you that are trying to make things worse should know that you're nobody to judge someone's marriage, decision and their personal matters even if one of them decided to publish it online. Destroying someone's life with so little evidence of wrong doing is just stupid.

Lastly, for those of you that shared his address and phone number and other private information, I hope someone will do that to you too. You can shame someone online, and he could be a terrible father and husband, but he is not a murder.

He is my friend, and a bandmate in the past. For some of you that say he can't play, you're wrong. We had a great time playing music together and he was good at the type of music that he liked. I never felt that he was unkind or unpleasant to be with in those years. He was playful and at most I would say that he used to enjoy the spotlight a bit more than I do. We haven't been in touch much since I left HK, but I do catch up with him once in a long while.

A lot of you were trying to get him fired, but you didn't think that getting him fired would not solve the problem. Getting him fired, would actually make things worse for the mother and the kid, but you did it anyway. Look, I'm not trying to convince anyone on who is right or wrong, but if you read their conversation, certainly there is more to the story than that $5000 or "cheating". There is the hacking which seems to have stressed him up, then there is their relationship issue which is really hard for outsider to judge.

People insert lots of information about him cheating, about how he was behaving at work, or how he DM girls on Instagram, and I don't know if they are all real because I couldn't picture him like that and you're not trying to prove it. If everything is true tho, he is a terrible person. However, all the messages I've read are either "my friend told me..." Or "I heard that he....". This is just some BS. Let's talk about facts. Moreover, without saying it is their fault, the girls that knowingly cheat with him are doing so willingly.

I haven't spoke to him yet, but I hope he can hang on, and for those of you that are trying to make things worse should know that you're nobody to judge someone's marriage, decision and their personal matters even if one of them decided to publish it online. Destroying someone's life with so little evidence of wrong doing is just stupid.

Lastly, for those of you that shared his address and phone number and other private information, I hope someone will do that to you too. You can shame someone online, and he could be a terrible father and husband, but he is not a murder.

I never felt that he was unkind or unpleasant to be with in those years. He was playful and at most I would say that he used to enjoy the spotlight a bit more than I do. We haven't been in touch much since I left HK, but I do catch up with him once in a long while.

A lot of you were trying to get him fired, but you didn't think that getting him fired would not solve the problem. Getting him fired, would actually make things worse for the mother and the kid, but you did it anyway. Look, I'm not trying to convince anyone on who is right or wrong, but if you read their conversation, certainly there is more to the story than that $5000 or "cheating". There is the hacking which seems to have stressed him up, then there is their relationship issue which is really hard for outsider to judge.

People insert lots of information about him cheating, about how he was behaving at work, or how he DM girls on Instagram, and I don't know if they are all real because I couldn't picture him like that and you're not trying to prove it. If everything is true tho, he is a terrible person. However, all the messages I've read are either "my friend told me..." Or "I heard that he....". This is just some BS. Let's talk about facts. Moreover, without saying it is their fault, the girls that knowingly cheat with him are doing so willingly.

I haven't spoke to him yet, but I hope he can hang on, and for those of you that are trying to make things worse should know that you're nobody to judge someone's marriage, decision and their personal matters even if one of them decided to publish it online. Destroying someone's life with so little evidence of wrong doing is just stupid.

Lastly, for those of you that shared his address and phone number and other private information, I hope someone will do that to you too. You can shame someone online, and he could be a terrible father and husband, but he is not a murder.

quote.msg:
He is my friend, and a bandmate in the past. For some of you that say he can't play, you're wrong. We had a great time playing music together and he was good at the type of music that he liked. I never felt that he was unkind or unpleasant to be with in those years. He was playful and at most I would say that he used to enjoy the spotlight a bit more than I do. We haven't been in touch much since I left HK, but I do catch up with him once in a long while.

A lot of you were trying to get him fired, but you didn't think that getting him fired would not solve the problem. Getting him fired, would actually make things worse for the mother and the kid, but you did it anyway. Look, I'm not trying to convince anyone on who is right or wrong, but if you read their conversation, certainly there is more to the story than that $5000 or "cheating". There is the hacking which seems to have stressed him up, then there is their relationship issue which is really hard for outsider to judge.

People insert lots of information about him cheating, about how he was behaving at work, or how he DM girls on Instagram, and I don't know if they are all real because I couldn't picture him like that and you're not trying to prove it. If everything is true tho, he is a terrible person. However, all the messages I've read are either "my friend told me..." Or "I heard that he....". This is just some BS. Let's talk about facts. Moreover, without saying it is their fault, the girls that knowingly cheat with him are doing so willingly.

I haven't spoke to him yet, but I hope he can hang on, and for those of you that are trying to make things worse should know that you're nobody to judge someone's marriage, decision and their personal matters even if one of them decided to publish it online. Destroying someone's life with so little evidence of wrong doing is just stupid.

Lastly, for those of you that shared his address and phone number and other private information, I hope someone will do that to you too. You can shame someone online, and he could be a terrible father and husband, but he is not a murder.

quote.msg:
He is my friend, and a bandmate in the past. For some of you that say he can't play, you're wrong. We had a great time playing music together and he was good at the type of music that he liked. I never felt that he was unkind or unpleasant to be with in those years. He was playful and at most I would say that he used to enjoy the spotlight a bit more than I do. We haven't been in touch much since I left HK, but I do catch up with him once in a long while.

A lot of you were trying to get him fired, but you didn't think that getting him fired would not solve the problem. Getting him fired, would actually make things worse for the mother and the kid, but you did it anyway. Look, I'm not trying to convince anyone on who is right or wrong, but if you read their conversation, certainly there is more to the story than that $5000 or "cheating". There is the hacking which seems to have stressed him up, then there is their relationship issue which is really hard for outsider to judge.

People insert lots of information about him cheating, about how he was behaving at work, or how he DM girls on Instagram, and I don't know if they are all real because I couldn't picture him like that and you're not trying to prove it. If everything is true tho, he is a terrible person. However, all the messages I've read are either "my friend told me..." Or "I heard that he....". This is just some BS. Let's talk about facts. Moreover, without saying it is their fault, the girls that knowingly cheat with him are doing so willingly.

I haven't spoke to him yet, but I hope he can hang on, and for those of you that are trying to make things worse should know that you're nobody to judge someone's marriage, decision and their personal matters even if one of them decided to publish it online. Destroying someone's life with so little evidence of wrong doing is just stupid.

Lastly, for those of you that shared his address and phone number and other private information, I hope someone will do that to you too. You can shame someone online, and he could be a terrible father and husband, but he is not a murder.
